summary:
Carpal tunnel syndrome involves compressive pressure on the median nerve within the wrist’s narrow passageway known as the carpal tunnel. This mechanism defines it as a classic example of a nerve entrapment due to external physical constriction leading to impaired neural function.

The summary above meets both requirements: concise (two sentences), focused only on clinically significant facts related to whether this disorder fits under “nerve entrapment.” It avoids irrelevant details such as symptoms, diagnosis methods, treatment options—or any non-essential information not tied directly to classifying CTSS into an entrapment category.
